title: PE renormalization
summary: Aggregate import for Laurent/log expansions, finite parts, cutoff functionals, renormalized volume, curvature integrals, and divergence vanishing.

mathematicalContent.heading: Renormalized quantities
mathematicalContent.body: The imported modules model a cutoff quantity by a finite Laurent expansion, a logarithmic term, and a constant term. The constant term is the finite part or renormalized value, while the log coefficient records the anomaly.

mathematicalContent.heading: What this file proves
mathematicalContent.body: Renormalization.lean itself contains only six imports and no declarations. The precise interfaces for finite parts, volumes, curvature integrals, and divergence cancellation live in the child modules.

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by the imported modules.
Background for conformally compact metrics and their asymptotic expansions.

limitations: This is an import-only aggregate. The displayed asymptotic formula explains the common mathematical theme but is not a theorem declared in Renormalization.lean.
